markets.question: New Rihanna Album before GTA VI?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Rihanna officially releases a new album before Grand Theft Auto VI is officially released in the US. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If neither occurs by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to 50-50.

For the purposes of this market, "release" refers to the game becoming publicly available for purchase or download in the US. Early access, beta versions, other forms of pre-release availability, or leaks will not count as an official release. If the release is only for certain consoles (e.g. Xbox Series X/S) it will count.

The resolution source for the release of GTA VI is official information from Rockstar Games or its parent company, Take-Two Interactive.

Officially released means that a newly released album is officially available for download or streaming (not including live events) by the resolution date.

Re-releases, deluxe editions, compilations, or greatest hits albums will not count, unless they contain at least 50% tracks that have never been officially released in any format prior to the album's release.

The resolution source for the album release will be any official streaming or download site, e.g. Apple Music or Spotify.

events.title: What will happen before GTA VI?
events.description: This is a market on predicting events that will occur before the release of Grand Theft Auto VI.

eventMetadata.context_description: GTA VI’s confirmed November 19, 2026 release date, following two official delays from an initial 2025 window to May and then November 2026 for additional polish, has extended the timeline and sharpened trader focus on events that could precede launch. Rockstar and parent Take-Two have reiterated the date remains firm in recent earnings commentary, while the delay has reshaped the broader 2026 game calendar by pushing competitors away from the fall window. Marketing momentum is expected to build through the summer with a potential third trailer, pre-order campaigns, and streaming metrics that historically drive cultural conversation around major Rockstar titles. These factors, combined with the game’s projected record-breaking first-year sales, create a wide window for developments in music releases, AI advancements, and political shifts to influence market-implied odds before resolution.

markets.question: Will Harvey Weinstein be sentenced to no prison time?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the prison sentence, if any, imposed on Harvey Weinstein as part of his ongoing New York retrial (People of the State of New York v. Harvey Weinstein) by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve immediately based on the first sentence rendered in this case, regardless of any appeals. If Weinstein is found not guilty, there is a mistrial, or if the first sentencing does not include any jail or prison time, this market will resolve to "No Prison Time."

If no sentencing takes place by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will also resolve to "No Prison Time."

If the sentence falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

For the purposes of this market, it does not matter whether the sentence is concurrent or consecutive to any existing sentence Weinstein is already serving. The market will resolve based on the total prison sentence imposed in this case.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the New York court system or other involved U.S. government sources; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent mistrial in Harvey Weinstein’s third New York retrial on May 15, 2026, after a deadlocked jury, has reinforced trader consensus around No Prison Time at 80% implied probability. The disgraced producer’s original 2020 New York conviction was overturned on appeal, and prosecutors now face a June 24 hearing on whether to pursue yet another retrial for the same charges. Meanwhile, Weinstein continues serving his separate 16-year California sentence while his legal team appeals that verdict on similar procedural grounds. At 74, with prolonged incarceration since 2020 and ongoing appeals creating realistic pathways for reduced or vacated time, markets reflect limited near-term risk of substantial additional years. Upcoming rulings on retrial and California appeal remain the key catalysts.

markets.question: Will the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve to “No” if it becomes impossible for this team to win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup based off the rules of the NHL.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the NHL.

events.title: 2026 NHL Stanley Cup Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NHL Stanley Cup championship.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Carolina Hurricanes hold a narrow edge in the 2026 Stanley Cup Final against the Vegas Golden Knights, with the best-of-seven series tied at one game apiece after Carolina rallied from a two-goal deficit for a 4-3 overtime victory in Game 2. The Hurricanes posted the NHL’s second-best regular-season record at 53-22-7 and advanced through the Eastern Conference with strong defensive structure and special-teams play, while the Golden Knights reached their second Final in franchise history by overcoming deficits in the Western Conference playoffs. Recent momentum favors Carolina after erasing an early series deficit, though Vegas demonstrated offensive firepower in its Game 1 road win. Trader consensus reflects these factors alongside home-ice considerations and historical series trends in closely matched Finals.

events.title: 2026 NBA Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NBA Finals.

eventMetadata.context_description: The New York Knicks' 105-95 road victory in Game 1 of the NBA Finals has shifted slight momentum in the 2026 championship market, elevating their implied probability to 52.4% while the San Antonio Spurs sit at 47.3%. Both teams arrived with strong regular-season foundations—the Spurs at 62-20 and the Knicks at 53-29—featuring deep rotations anchored by Jalen Brunson's clutch scoring and Karl-Anthony Towns' interior presence for New York, alongside Victor Wembanyama's two-way dominance and supporting cast for San Antonio. With the series shifting to Game 2 in San Antonio before moving to Madison Square Garden, the tight pricing reflects evenly matched contenders where home-court edges, recent form, and potential adjustments in a best-of-seven format keep outcomes highly competitive.

markets.question: Will the San Antonio Spurs win the 2026 NBA Finals?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the San Antonio Spurs win the 2026 NBA Finals. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve to “No” if it becomes impossible for this team to win the 2026 NBA Finals based off the rules of the NBA.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the NBA.

events.title: 2026 NBA Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NBA Finals.

eventMetadata.context_description: The New York Knicks' 105-95 road victory in Game 1 of the NBA Finals has shifted slight momentum in the 2026 championship market, elevating their implied probability to 52.4% while the San Antonio Spurs sit at 47.3%. Both teams arrived with strong regular-season foundations—the Spurs at 62-20 and the Knicks at 53-29—featuring deep rotations anchored by Jalen Brunson's clutch scoring and Karl-Anthony Towns' interior presence for New York, alongside Victor Wembanyama's two-way dominance and supporting cast for San Antonio. With the series shifting to Game 2 in San Antonio before moving to Madison Square Garden, the tight pricing reflects evenly matched contenders where home-court edges, recent form, and potential adjustments in a best-of-seven format keep outcomes highly competitive.

markets.question: Will MegaETH perform an airdrop by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if MegaETH launches a token and performs an airdrop by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market "locked" tokens or non-swappable tokens will not suffice to resolve this market to "Yes". Airdrops of NFTs will not qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market is on-chain information and official information from MegaETH, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

events.description: this is a market on MegaETH airdrop

markets.question: Will Spain win the 2026 FIFA World Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Spain and France share the narrowest edge in the World Cup winner market as the expanded 48-team tournament approaches kickoff, driven by their elite squad depth, recent major-tournament pedigree, and consistent high-level form. Spain enters as European champions with a youthful core featuring standout attackers and midfield control, while France draws on back-to-back final appearances and a star-laden roster anchored by proven defenders and creators. England sits close behind with its own talented attack and experience from consecutive European finals, creating a competitive top tier where small edges in fitness or draw positioning can shift implied probabilities. South American contenders trail due to comparatively less recent cohesion among the leaders, though their historical pedigree keeps them in contention.

England sits close behind with its own talented attack and experience from consecutive European finals, creating a competitive top tier where small edges in fitness or draw positioning can shift implied probabilities. South American contenders trail due to comparatively less recent cohesion among the leaders, though their historical pedigree keeps them in contention.

markets.question: Will Ecuador win the 2026 FIFA World Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

markets.question: Xi Jinping out before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if China's General Secretary of the Communist Party, Xi Jinping, is removed from power for any length of time between July 3, 2025, and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

CCP General Secretary Xi Jinping will be considered removed from power if he announces his resignation from his role as General Secretary, or is otherwise dismissed, detained, disqualified, or otherwise loses his position or is prevented from fulfilling his duties as General Secretary within this market's timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Xi Jinping's removal of presidential term limits in 2018 and his third-term confirmation as CCP General Secretary at the 20th Party Congress in 2022 have entrenched his authority, with preparations for a fourth term at the 21st Congress in late 2027 proceeding under his direction. Recent developments, including his leadership of planning for the 2026-2030 Five-Year Plan, a forward-looking 2026 New Year address, and Central Committee reshuffles alongside targeted military purges, have reinforced elite alignment without signs of internal challenge or diminished control through mid-2026. These factors underpin the strong trader consensus on the "No" outcome. Late-breaking health events or unprecedented elite fractures remain the primary scenarios that could alter probabilities before the end of 2026.

markets.question: Will Gavin Newsom win the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: The 2028 Democratic presidential nomination remains highly fragmented, with California Governor Gavin Newsom holding the top trader-implied probability due to his statewide executive experience and national visibility as a potential unifying figure. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez draws backing from the progressive wing, Kamala Harris from prior national exposure, and Jon Ossoff and Josh Shapiro from Senate and gubernatorial records in competitive states. The dispersed pricing across dozens of outcomes underscores uncertainty over which candidate can assemble a durable coalition ahead of primaries. Key consolidation factors include 2026 midterm performance, early fundraising momentum, endorsements from party leaders, and polling trends in battleground states, consistent with historical patterns where name recognition and institutional support often shift probabilities as the cycle advances.
